Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor

Class MdcAsyncListener

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.MdcAsyncListener
public final class MdcAsyncListener extends Object
Bridges SLF4J MDC context from the request (dispatch) thread to the CompletableFuture completion thread.
Problem

When a @RestOp method returns a CompletableFuture, the AsyncResponseProcessor registers a whenComplete callback that runs on whatever thread completes the future — typically a different thread from the one that processed the request. SLF4J's MDC is backed by a ThreadLocal, so any diagnostic context set by an upstream filter (e.g. requestId, userId) is invisible to log statements emitted inside that callback.

Solution

MdcAsyncListener provides a single factory method, wrap(BiConsumer, Map), that wraps a whenComplete callback with the standard MDC snapshot-restore-clear pattern:

  1. Snapshot the request thread's MDC map via MDC.getCopyOfContextMap() at dispatch time.
  2. On entry to the completion callback: save the completion thread's current MDC (if any) and install the request-thread snapshot via MDC.setContextMap(snapshot).
  3. Callback body runs with the request-thread MDC visible.
  4. In the finally block: restore the completion thread's original MDC state (if it had one) or call MDC.clear() — this removes exactly the keys the listener installed without disturbing any MDC state the completion thread itself had before the callback.
Lazy skip

snapshot() returns null when the request thread's MDC is empty or when SLF4J is absent. wrap(BiConsumer, Map) returns the original callback unchanged when the snapshot is null, so no work is done on the completion thread for the common case of no MDC state.

SLF4J detection

MdcAsyncListener uses Class.forName(String) to probe for org.slf4j.MDC at class-load time. SLF4J is NOT a compile-time dependency of juneau-rest-server. If SLF4J is absent from the runtime classpath, the class gracefully degrades: isAvailable() returns false, snapshot() returns null, and wrap(BiConsumer, Map) returns the original callback.

Limitation

This class bridges SLF4J MDC only. Users of Log4j2's ThreadContext directly (without the SLF4J facade) will not benefit from this listener. That is documented as a v1 limitation; the SLF4J MDC is the lingua franca for structured logging context propagation in the JVM ecosystem.

Since:
10.0.0
See Also:
  • AsyncResponseProcessor
  • RestContext.isMdcAsyncPropagation()

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static boolean
    isAvailable()
    Returns whether SLF4J MDC is available on the runtime classpath.

    Takes a snapshot of the current thread's SLF4J MDC map.
    static <T> BiConsumer<T,Throwable>

  • Method Details

    • isAvailable

      public static boolean isAvailable()
      Returns whether SLF4J MDC is available on the runtime classpath.

      When false, all methods on this class are no-ops.

      Returns:
      true if org.slf4j.MDC was found at class-load time.

    • snapshot

      public static Map<String,String> snapshot()
      Takes a snapshot of the current thread's SLF4J MDC map.

      Call this on the request/dispatch thread before submitting async work. The returned map is passed to wrap(BiConsumer, Map) to decorate the completion callback.

      Returns null when:

      • SLF4J is absent from the classpath, or
      • The current thread's MDC is null or empty (lazy-skip contract — no work is done on the completion thread when the request had no MDC context).
      Returns:
      A copy of the current MDC map, or null.

    • wrap

      public static <T> BiConsumer<T,Throwable> wrap(BiConsumer<T,Throwable> action, Map<String,String> requestMdc)
      Wraps a BiConsumer (typically a whenComplete callback) so the completion thread sees the request thread's MDC context.

      The wrapping logic:

      1. Save the completion thread's current MDC map (usually null for pool / virtual threads).
      2. Install requestMdc on the completion thread via MDC.setContextMap(requestMdc).
      3. Invoke action inside a try block.
      4. finally: if the completion thread had its own MDC, restore it; otherwise call MDC.clear() — either way only the keys the listener installed are removed.

      If requestMdc is null (e.g. request thread had no MDC, or SLF4J absent), the original action is returned unchanged — zero overhead on the completion thread.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The future result type.
      Parameters:
      action - The callback to wrap.
      requestMdc - The MDC snapshot taken on the dispatch thread (from snapshot()), or null.
      Returns:
      A wrapped callback, or action itself when wrapping is not needed.
